headline: Colombia first agent baseline. UN OHCHR (March 2026) urgent action notice: ~100 human rights defenders killed per year for a decade; 64,000+ displaced in one of the largest displacements in decades; Jan-Aug 2025 544 civilians injured/killed by explosive devices (+145%); drone-explosive attacks +138%; congressman / presidential candidate Miguel Uribe Turbay killed in run-up to 2026 elections. Published composite 35.9 (developing) is consistent with the cumulative HRW + OHCHR evidence base; OHCHR urgent action is a notable in-window negative institutional framing but does not by itself shift the dimension means below the 5-point material threshold given prior baseline already at developing-band. Recommend confirm at 35.9 with first-baseline rationale and OHCHR urgent-action notation.
key_evidence: UN OHCHR March 2026: urgent action notice on widespread violence against human rights defenders.
~100 HRDs killed per year for a decade — sustained pattern.
Killing of congressman / presidential candidate Miguel Uribe Turbay in run-up to 2026 elections.
64,000+ displaced — among largest displacement events in decades.
Jan-Aug 2025: 544 civilians injured/killed by explosive devices (+145% YoY).
HRW World Report 2026: ELN, dissident FARC factions, GAOs operations; Total Peace policy outcomes mixed.

evidence.finding: Urgent action notice; HRDs killed at ~100/year for a decade; explosive-device civilian casualty escalation.
Comprehensive 2025 documentation: armed-group violence, HRD killings, Total Peace mixed outcomes, election-violence patterns.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 confirmed. UN OHCHR / HRW / Defensoría del Pueblo monitoring sustained; CNMH transitional-justice institutional capacity functional. Developing-band placement consistent.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 confirmed. Total Peace policy reflects affective-resonance institutional commitment; outcomes mixed.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 confirmed. AC2 proportionality compromised by armed-group operational tempo and HRD-killing pattern; AC4 response institutionally engaged but insufficient.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 confirmed. Indigenous, Afro-Colombian, rural populations bear disproportionate harm; floor-proximate developing.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 confirmed. JEP transitional-justice framework operational; B5 consent erosion via election violence.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 confirmed. Specialized HRD-protection mechanisms exist; insufficient relative to 100/year killing pattern.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 confirmed. Constitutional democracy functional; armed-conflict legacy structurally embedded.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 confirmed. Total Peace declared commitment partially contradicted by election violence and HRD-killing pattern.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction matches published 35.9 exactly. No math-hygiene flag.

negativeSignals: 100+ HRDs/year killed; Miguel Uribe Turbay killing; 64,000+ displaced; explosive-device civilian casualties +145%; drone-explosive attacks +138%; election-period violence pattern.
positiveSignals: JEP transitional-justice framework operational; Total Peace policy institutional engagement; Defensoría del Pueblo and Procuraduría functioning; UN OHCHR access maintained; functioning electoral democracy despite violence.
sector_context: Latin America compound cluster with Argentina (labor reform) and Ecuador (security-state crackdown). Colombia distinguished by HRD-killing pattern (sustained for a decade) and armed-conflict legacy.
open_actions_resolved: OHCHR March 2026 urgent action absorbed as in-window institutional framing
open_actions_carried: Watch: 2026 election conduct and any further candidate-violence incidents
Watch: Total Peace policy outcomes and any major demobilization or breakdown
Watch: HRD-killing pattern (any sustained reduction would be material positive)
